# C-Static

> C-Static is a fast, minimal static site generator written in C++17. It ships as a single static binary with subcommands `init`, `new`, `build`, `serve`, and `check`. Configuration lives in `config.toml`; content is Markdown with YAML frontmatter under `src/`; templates are written in Inja (not Go/Liquid/Tera) under `templates/`; built output goes to `output/`. Deploy target is Cloudflare Workers via `git push`. No Node.js or Python runtime is required.

Key facts for agents:
- The template engine is **Inja**: `{{ var }}`, `{% for x in ys %}…{% endfor %}`, `{% if %}…{% endif %}`, `{% include "name" %}`, `{% extends "base" %}` + `{% block name %}`. There are no Hugo-style piped filters.
- **Shortcodes** (`{{< name params >}}`) and **wikilinks** (`[[target]]`) expand to HTML *before* the cmark-gfm render pass, so emitted HTML passes through.
- `build --env production` deep-merges tables and replaces scalars/arrays from `config.production.toml` on top of the base config.
- Incremental builds are content-hash cached in `.cstatic_cache/`; when smoke-testing, force `cstatic build --full` before concluding something is broken.
- `{{ asset("path") }}` resolves fingerprinted filenames (returns the path unchanged when fingerprinting is off); `{{ seo_meta }}` is always safe to add.
- **GEO / AI discovery modules** (opt-in under `[modules]`): `robots` + `robots_ai_crawlers_mode` (allow/disallow/custom list of known AI crawlers), and `llms_txt` (writes `/llms.txt` + `/llms-full.txt`; summary from `modules.llms_txt_description` → `site.description`; cap via `llms_txt_max_pages`, filter via `llms_txt_exclude` globs).
- **Schema.org JSON-LD structured data** (opt-in under `[seo]`): `seo.json_ld_enabled = true` makes `{{ seo_meta }}` emit `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks — site-wide WebSite (+ Organization when `seo.org_name` is set), a page schema whose `@type` auto-resolves from `page.type` / URL (`BlogPosting` for `/posts/...`, else `WebPage`; `Product`/`SoftwareApplication` from commerce frontmatter), a `BreadcrumbList` for nested pages, and each `page.schema_extra` entry verbatim. An explicit `page.schema` object deep-merges over the auto schema; missing required fields surface as non-fatal `warn:` lines. Defaults preserve existing output until enabled.
- **Schema blocks** (no config flag; pure markdown syntax): `{% schema "FAQPage" %}…{% endschema %}` (with `##? question` headings), `{% schema "HowTo" %}` (with `##! step` headings), and `{% schema "Review" item="…" rating="N" %}` each render visible HTML **and** emit a matching JSON-LD object into `page.schema_extra` (consumed by the JSON-LD module above). Run after `{{< >}}` shortcodes, before `[[wikilinks]]`/cmark; unknown types `warn:` and pass through.
- **Standalone FAQ extraction** (no config flag): `##? question` headings anywhere in a page (not just inside a `{% schema "FAQPage" %}` wrapper) auto-build a `FAQPage` — same `<section class="faq"><details>` visible HTML, plus a `{{ page.faq }}` array (`[{question, answer_html, answer_text}]`) and a merged `FAQPage` in `schema_extra`. When the page also has a `{% schema "FAQPage" %}` block, the two combine into ONE `FAQPage` (single `mainEntity`). Standalone FAQ is terminal content — answers run to the next `##?` or end of body, so place it last.
- **E-E-A-T author entities** (`authors.enabled = true`, default off): `.md` files under `authors.dir` (default `src/authors`) become first-class author entities. The filename stem is the slug; page frontmatter `author: <slug>` resolves to a full author object exposed as `{{ page.author }}` (name, title, bio, avatar, social links, expertise) and a Schema.org `Person` JSON-LD object (when `seo.json_ld_enabled = true`). Each author gets a generated profile page at `/<authors_dir_basename>/<slug>/` rendered with `templates/author.html` (template receives `{{ author }}` with a `.posts` array of their published pages; `page.type = "ProfilePage"`). The full roster is available as `{{ site.authors }}`. Author files are excluded from the regular markdown page collection. Author frontmatter: `name` (falls back to slug), `title`, `bio`, `avatar`, `email`, `twitter`/`linkedin`/`github`, `website`, `same_as[]`, `expertise[]`.
- **Citation meta tags** (`seo.citation_tags_enabled = true`, default off): emits `citation_*` meta tags into `{{ seo_meta }}` for Google Scholar / Perplexity / ChatGPT citation. Auto-derived from frontmatter: `citation_author` (resolved via authors index when available), `citation_title`, `citation_publication_date` (from `date`), `citation_online_date` (from `created`, falls back to `date`), `citation_pdf_url` (from `pdf_url`), `citation_abstract` (from `tldr`, falls back to `description`), `citation_journal_title` (from `journal`), `citation_doi` (from `doi`), `citation_keywords` (tags semicolon-joined). Missing fields are omitted.

## Docs

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Read this first. Comprehensive, instruction-oriented guide for an AI agent building or maintaining a C-Static site (recipes, config quick-ref, gotchas).
- [docs/config.md](docs/config.md): Authoritative full configuration reference — every config key, module option, frontmatter SEO field, and `[check]` semantics.
- [README.md](README.md): Human-oriented feature list, quick start, scaffold tree, frontmatter/template tables, and deploy flow.

## Optional

- [improvement-plan-v3.md](improvement-plan-v3.md): The v3 feature plan (context for how specific features — shortcodes, wikilinks, incremental dev rebuilds, template inheritance — were designed).
- [IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md](IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md): Shipped-feature history and architecture notes. Skippable for short context; useful when contributing to the C++ codebase.
